spec_match.rationale: Enumerates the full event set the spec defines (loadstart/loadcommit/loadstop, newwindow, permissionrequest, dialog, download, findupdate, consolemessage, unresponsive, responsive, zoomchange, exit).

interactive.rationale: Six scenario buttons that each fire the corresponding event(s) with payloads, plus a counter chip array and a timestamped log.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises every event with counters and one-click simulations; the page delivers that.

edge_cases.rationale: Happy-path simulations only. Doesn't show: e.preventDefault failure (event already preventDefault'd by another listener), permissionrequest with allow-async then deny, dialog with messageType=confirm and a cancellation.

references.rationale: Includes the correct chromestatus.com/feature/5199572022853632 link and the spec event table.

openQuestions.title: Show prevent-default failure modes
openQuestions.detail: Adding `dialog` interception requires `e.dialog.ok()` or `e.dialog.cancel()` — forgetting that hangs the embedded page indefinitely. The router should include a 'do nothing' scenario that demonstrates the bug, with a fix variant alongside.

openQuestions.title: Pair each event with its async resolution
openQuestions.detail: `permissionrequest` exposes `e.request.allowUntilSessionEnds()` and similar in newer drafts. Currently the demo only shows `allow()` / `deny()`. Capture the full grant surface so authors know what's available.

summary: Strong event-surface tour. Each event named, counted, and simulated. To level up, show failure modes (forgetting to resolve a dialog, double-preventDefault) and the full grant surface.
